entries.object_class: Large cardinal (Measurable cardinal with normal ultrafilter and Mitchell order)

object_specification.type: Measurable cardinal kappa with a specified normal kappa-complete non-principal ultrafilter U; Mitchell order o(kappa) = 0 specified

instance.axiom_statement: exists kappa exists U (kappa uncountable AND U is a kappa-complete non-principal ultrafilter on kappa AND U is normal: for any regressive f: kappa -> kappa with {alpha : f(alpha) < alpha} in U, there exists beta and X in U with X subseteq f^{-1}({beta}))

explicit_ultrafilter_specification.filter_axioms: (i) kappa in U, (ii) X, Y in U => X cap Y in U, (iii) X in U, X subseteq Y => Y in U, (iv) emptyset not in U
explicit_ultrafilter_specification.kappa_completeness: intersection of fewer than kappa many U-sets is in U
explicit_ultrafilter_specification.normality: diagonal-intersection closure: if X_alpha in U for alpha < kappa, then Delta_{alpha < kappa} X_alpha = {beta : for all alpha < beta, beta in X_alpha} is in U

instance.mitchell_order: o(kappa) = 0 means there is no normal measure on kappa concentrating on {alpha < kappa : alpha measurable}
instance.elementary_embedding: j: V -> M = Ult(V, U) with crit(j) = kappa, j(kappa) > kappa, M^kappa subseteq M (kappa-closure)

structural_metadata.consistent_relative_to_ZFC: strict consistency strength above ZFC; equiconsistent with 'kappa measurable + 0# exists'

verification_methods.ultrafilter_axioms: check the four filter axioms, kappa-completeness, non-principality, normality on the explicit U
verification_methods.embedding_construction: build Ult(V, U) and verify crit(j) = kappa via Los's theorem
verification_methods.M_closure: verify [f]_U for f: kappa -> V, kappa-sequences are absorbed
verification_methods.mitchell_order_zero: show {alpha < kappa : alpha measurable in V_kappa} not in U

instance.comparison: any two iterates compare via further iteration to a common iterate (Kunen)

object_specification.type: Gamma_0 = least alpha such that phi_alpha(0) = alpha; the proof-theoretic ordinal of predicative analysis (ATR_0)

instance.veblen_function_definition: phi_0(alpha) = omega^alpha; for beta > 0, phi_beta(alpha) is the alpha-th common fixed point of {phi_gamma : gamma < beta} (i.e., the alpha-th point fixed by all lower phi's)
instance.diagonal_iteration: Gamma_0 = sup_n Gamma_0^{(n)} where Gamma_0^{(0)} = 0 and Gamma_0^{(n+1)} = phi_{Gamma_0^{(n)}}(0)
instance.ordinal_notation: Veblen normal form: every alpha < Gamma_0 is uniquely alpha = phi_{beta_1}(alpha_1) + ... + phi_{beta_k}(alpha_k) with the standard descending-additive constraints; recursive comparison defined by case analysis on Veblen forms
instance.proof_theoretic_role: Gamma_0 is the proof-theoretic ordinal of ATR_0 (arithmetic transfinite recursion) and of predicative analysis (Feferman 1964, Schutte 1965)

structural_metadata.is_marker_for_predicativity: Gamma_0 marks the boundary of Schutte's predicative analysis

entries.object_class: Large cardinal (Supercompact cardinal via Magidor's elementary-embedding characterization)

object_specification.type: kappa is supercompact iff for every lambda >= kappa there is an elementary embedding j: V -> M with crit(j) = kappa, j(kappa) > lambda, and M^lambda subseteq M

instance.axiom_statement: for all lambda exists j, M (j: V -> M elementary, crit(j) = kappa, j(kappa) > lambda, M is closed under lambda-sequences (i.e., every f: lambda -> M with f in V is in M))
instance.ultrafilter_characterization: equivalent: for every lambda >= kappa there is a normal fine kappa-complete ultrafilter U on P_kappa(lambda) where P_kappa(lambda) = {x subseteq lambda : |x| < kappa}
instance.fineness_definition: U is fine iff for every alpha < lambda, {x in P_kappa(lambda) : alpha in x} in U
instance.magidor_characterization: kappa supercompact iff for every alpha there is a beta < kappa and an elementary embedding i: V_beta -> V_alpha with i(crit(i)) = kappa (Magidor 1971)
instance.explicit_lambda_lambda_supercompact_witn: for lambda = kappa^+, the ultrafilter U_lambda on P_kappa(kappa^+) is normal, fine, kappa-complete; for the embedding j_U: V -> Ult(V, U), crit(j_U) = kappa and Ult is closed under kappa^+-sequences

structural_metadata.consistent_relative_to_ZFC: very strong: implies measurable, strong, Woodin, and many more
structural_metadata.preserves_under_small_forcing: Laver-prepared supercompactness is indestructible under <kappa-directed-closed forcing

verification_methods.embedding_existence: for each lambda, exhibit U_lambda normal+fine+kappa-complete on P_kappa(lambda) and construct Ult(V, U_lambda)
verification_methods.fineness_check: for each alpha < lambda, verify {x : alpha in x} is in U_lambda
verification_methods.lambda_closure: verify M = Ult is closed under lambda-sequences via factoring through a kappa-internal lambda-sequence
verification_methods.magidor_equivalence: show Magidor characterization equivalent via reflection of supercompactness witnesses

object_specification.type: Coupled-rotator interacting particle system with explicit pairwise sinusoidal coupling and mean-field N -> infinity limit

instance.mean_field_McKean_Vlasov_PDE: partial_t rho(theta, omega, t) + partial_theta ((omega + K * Im(e^{-i*theta} * r(t))) * rho) = 0, where r(t) = integral e^{i*theta} rho(theta, omega, t) d theta d omega is the order parameter

instance.K_critical_formula: K_c = 2 / (pi * g(0)) for symmetric unimodal g; for standard Cauchy g(0) = 1/pi so K_c = 2

object_specification.type: Magidor forcing P_M(kappa, lambda) for changing cof(kappa) to lambda < kappa, using a coherent sequence of measures

instance.parameters: kappa with a coherent sequence of normal measures U = <U_alpha : alpha < lambda> where U_alpha concentrates on {beta < kappa : o(beta) = alpha} (Mitchell order)
instance.underlying_set: conditions are pairs (f, F) where f is a partial function from lambda to kappa with |dom(f)| < lambda, increasing on its domain, and F is a function on lambda \ dom(f) such that F(alpha) in U_alpha for every alpha in dom(F), and the F-image is bounded compatibly with f
instance.order: (g, G) <= (f, F) iff dom(f) subseteq dom(g), g restricted to dom(f) = f, for alpha in dom(g) \ dom(f) we have g(alpha) in F(alpha), and G(alpha) subseteq F(alpha) for alpha in dom(G) cap dom(F)
instance.maximum_element: (empty, F_max) where F_max(alpha) = {beta < kappa : o(beta) = alpha}
instance.coherence_requirement: the ultrafilter sequence must be coherent: if j_alpha : V -> M_alpha is the embedding from U_alpha then j_alpha(U) restricted to alpha equals U restricted to alpha

structural_metadata.consistency_strength: o(kappa) >= lambda + 1 (Mitchell order at least lambda+1)

verification_methods.prikry_like: for any sentence phi and (f, F), there exists F' <= F (pointwise in U) such that (f, F') decides phi

entries.object_class: Large cardinal (Reinhardt cardinal: ZF-consistent, ZFC-inconsistent by Kunen)

object_specification.type: Reinhardt cardinal kappa: critical point of a non-trivial elementary embedding j: V -> V (in ZF without choice)

instance.axiom_statement_ZF: exists j (j: V -> V elementary, j is not the identity, crit(j) = kappa, kappa is least with j(kappa) > kappa)
instance.kunen_inconsistency: ZFC + Reinhardt is inconsistent: Kunen's theorem (1971) shows there is no non-trivial j: V -> V in ZFC, via the Erdos-Hajnal partition theorem applied to a kappa-omega-sequence above the embedding's critical point, using AC at omega-th iterate
instance.kunen_proof_sketch: let lambda = sup_n j^n(kappa); by Erdos-Hajnal in ZFC there is f: [lambda]^omega -> lambda with no homogeneous set of size lambda; but j(f) restricted to a generic omega-sequence in j^omega(kappa) yields one, contradiction
instance.weaker_consistent_versions: I0, I1, I2, I3 axioms are ZFC-consistent and hover just below Reinhardt
instance.ZF_consistent_status: open whether ZF + Reinhardt is consistent; conjectured by some to be inconsistent; no known ZF-proof of inconsistency

structural_metadata.consistent_with_ZF: open (no proof of inconsistency in ZF; existence is hypothetical)
structural_metadata.inconsistency_proof_uses: Axiom of Choice at one specific point (extracting a homogeneous set from Erdos-Hajnal)
weaker_alternatives: I0: there is j: L(V_{lambda+1}) -> L(V_{lambda+1}) with crit(j) < lambda
I2: j: V -> M with V_lambda subseteq M

verification_methods.kunen_inconsistency_check: given a candidate j: V -> V in ZFC, derive contradiction via Erdos-Hajnal at sup_n j^n(kappa)
verification_methods.I0_through_I3_consistency: relative to a huge cardinal; ZFC + I0 is believed consistent

object_specification.type: Function f(x) = |x|^alpha on B(0,1) in R^n

instance.W^{1,p}_norm_status: f in W^{1,p}(B(0,1)) iff alpha p > 0 - n + 1, i.e., 0.5 * p > -2, true for all p > 0; thus f in W^{1,p} for all p with derivative nabla f = 0.5 |x|^{-0.5} x/|x|; ||nabla f||_p = (0.5)^p integral_{B(0,1)} |x|^{-0.5 p} dx = (0.5)^p * (omega_{n-1}/(n - 0.5p)) for n - 0.5 p > 0, i.e., p < 2 n = 6
instance.specific_value_for_n=3, p=4: f not in W^{1,4} (the integral diverges; alpha = 0.5 is below the threshold)

structural_metadata.regularity_threshold: f in W^{1,p} iff p < 2n / (1 - 2 alpha) = 2*3/(1-1) = infinity at alpha = 1/2 borderline; revising: f in W^{1,p} iff alpha p > -(n - p), i.e., alpha + n/p > 1; at alpha = 0.5, n = 3, this gives p < 6
structural_metadata.Sobolev_embedding: W^{1,2} -> L^6 in R^3, so f is in L^6 since f(x) = |x|^{0.5}, |f|^6 = |x|^3 integrable on B(0,1)
structural_metadata.criticality: alpha = 0.5 saturates W^{1, 2n}; for alpha < 0.5 in n = 3 the function is NOT in W^{1, 6}

instance.definition: omega = {0, 1, 2, 3, ...} = {n : n is a finite von Neumann ordinal}; explicitly 0 = emptyset, n+1 = n cup {n}, omega = union_n n
instance.first_order_axioms: omega is the smallest set X such that emptyset in X and (y in X => y cup {y} in X)
instance.ordinal_arithmetic: omega + 1 = omega cup {omega}; omega * 2 = omega + omega; omega^2 = sup_n (omega * n); omega^omega = sup_n omega^n

object_specification.type: M(chi_{[-1,1]})(x) on R, where M is the (uncentered) Hardy-Littlewood maximal operator

instance.value_at_x=2: (2 - 1 + something) / 3 - actually for uncentered: M_uncentered f(2) = sup_{0 in I containing 2} (1/|I|) integral_I f = sup_{interval containing 2} (length of intersection with [-1, 1]) / (length of interval); the sup over intervals starting at 2 - epsilon and going through [-1, 1] gives (1/(2 - epsilon + 1)) which approaches 1/3 as epsilon -> 0; so M f(2) = 1/3 by limit
instance.value_at_x=10: M f(10) = 1/11 (interval [-1, 10] of length 11 contains [-1, 1] of length 2, ratio 2/11; alternatively [-1.something, 10] gives 2/11.something < 2/11; so sup is 2/11)
instance.general_formula: M f(x) = 1 if |x| <= 1; M f(x) = 2 / (|x| + 1) if |x| > 1 (uncentered); for centered M, the formula is 2 / (1 + |x|^2)^? - actually the centered form is min(1, (|x| + 1) / (2|x|)) for x outside [-1, 1] and 1 inside

structural_metadata.decay_at_infinity: M f(x) ~ 2/|x| as |x| -> infinity (matches integral of f over large interval)

object_specification.type: Aronszajn tree T on omega_1: tree of height omega_1, all levels countable, no uncountable branch (note: Aronszajn trees provably exist in ZFC; Suslin requires more)

instance.explicit_construction_via_finite_partial: Level T_alpha = {f : f is a finite partial injective function alpha -> Q with cofinal range in Q cap (-infty, q) for some q in Q}; specifically T_alpha consists of strictly-increasing finite-to-rationals partial injections with bounded sup
instance.tree_order: f <_T g iff f subseteq g (g extends f)
instance.construction_recursion: successor: T_{alpha+1} = {f cup {(alpha, q)} : f in T_alpha, q in Q, q > sup(range(f)), q < bound(f)+1}; limit alpha: T_alpha = {union_n f_n : (f_n) is a cofinal sequence of compatible elements with bounded suprema}
instance.specialization: T is special: T = union_n T_n where each T_n is an antichain (Q-rationals provide the specialization function)

verification_methods.no_uncountable_branch: any branch B of height omega_1 would project to a strictly-increasing function omega_1 -> Q with bounded sup at every countable stage, but Q is countable so B would force injection omega_1 -> Q (contradiction)
verification_methods.all_levels_countable: by construction; each level a subset of finite partial functions with bounded range so countable
verification_methods.specialization: the bound-of-range function f |-> bound(f) (after rounding to finite Q-precision) gives a countable partition into antichains

entries.object_class: Forcing notion (Prikry forcing for a measurable cardinal)

object_specification.type: Prikry forcing P_kappa = ({(s, A) : s in [kappa]^{<omega}, s strictly increasing, A in U, min(A) > max(s)}, <=_P) where U is a normal ultrafilter on measurable kappa

instance.parameter: kappa is measurable with normal ultrafilter U on kappa (assumed given)
instance.underlying_set: pairs (s, A) where s = (alpha_0 < alpha_1 < ... < alpha_{n-1}) is a finite increasing sequence from kappa and A in U with min(A) > alpha_{n-1} (or A = kappa if s = empty)
instance.order: (t, B) <=_P (s, A) iff s is an initial segment of t, B subseteq A, and {t(i) : i >= |s|} subseteq A
instance.prikry_property_statement: for any sentence phi in the forcing language and any (s, A), there is B subseteq A with B in U such that (s, B) decides phi

verification_methods.preserves_cardinals: Prikry property + U-normality imply no new bounded subsets of kappa, hence cardinals preserved (Prikry 1970)
verification_methods.changes_cofinality: the union of generic stems is an omega-sequence cofinal in kappa
verification_methods.prikry_property_check: for each (s, A) and phi, define A_0 = {alpha in A : exists B subseteq A setminus (alpha+1), B in U, (s ^ <alpha>, B) decides phi}; show A_0 in U by normality

entries.object_class: Tree (Suslin tree on omega_1, diamond-based construction)

object_specification.type: Suslin tree T on omega_1: a tree of height omega_1, all levels countable, with no uncountable chain and no uncountable antichain

instance.construction_under_diamond: assume <>_{omega_1}; let <S_alpha : alpha < omega_1> be a diamond sequence (each S_alpha subseteq alpha and for every X subseteq omega_1, {alpha : X cap alpha = S_alpha} is stationary)
instance.level_construction: Level T_0 = {<>}; level T_{alpha+1} = {x ^ <0>, x ^ <1> : x in T_alpha}; for limit alpha define T_alpha by selecting cofinal branches in T cap alpha by case-analysis on whether S_alpha codes an antichain through T cap alpha
instance.limit_step_specification: if S_alpha codes a maximal antichain A in T cap alpha, then for each x in T cap alpha extending some element of A pick a branch through T cap alpha containing x and put its limit in T_alpha; if S_alpha does not code an antichain, fill T_alpha with all cofinal branches
instance.underlying_set: T = union_{alpha < omega_1} T_alpha; each T_alpha countable
instance.tree_order: x <_T y iff x is a proper initial segment of y (T is a subtree of 2^{<omega_1})

verification_methods.no_uncountable_antichain: diamond ensures any potential uncountable antichain A is anticipated as S_alpha for stationary alpha; the limit step seals A at level alpha so no extension above alpha avoids A
verification_methods.no_uncountable_chain: any chain through T projects to a function omega_1 -> 2 of unbounded length, but levels are countable so cofinal chain forces uncountable antichain (contradiction by previous)
verification_methods.all_levels_countable: by construction (countable splitting at each level)

Need TransfiniteTree primitive with height: Ordinal, level_indexer: Ordinal -> Set, limit_step_strategy: Callable. Plus a DiamondSequence primitive parametrized by stationarity verifier.

entries.object_class: Computable structure (Kleene's O, the system of recursive ordinal notations)

object_specification.type: Kleene's O: the set of natural-number indices for recursive ordinals, with a partial order |O| extending well-foundedness

instance.definition_recursive: O is the smallest set such that (a) 1 in O with |1| = 0; (b) if a in O with |a| = alpha, then 2^a in O with |2^a| = alpha + 1; (c) if e is an index for a total computable function such that for all n, phi_e(n) in O and phi_e(n) <_O phi_e(n+1), then 3 * 5^e in O with |3 * 5^e| = sup_n |phi_e(n)|
instance.partial_order: a <_O b is the transitive closure of: (i) a <_O 2^a; (ii) for each n, phi_e(n) <_O 3 * 5^e
instance.ordinal_height: the supremum of {|a| : a in O} is omega_1^{CK}, the Church-Kleene ordinal (least non-recursive ordinal)
instance.explicit_index_examples: 1 codes 0; 2^1 codes 1; 2^{2^1} codes 2; 3 * 5^e codes omega when phi_e(n) = 2^{2^{...^1}} (n-fold tower); etc.
instance.non_uniqueness: |.| is well-defined but the index encoding is not unique: many distinct indices a, b in O have |a| = |b|

structural_metadata.computability_status: the set O is Pi_1_1-complete; not arithmetical
structural_metadata.uniformity: the function n |-> |n| (when defined) is hyperarithmetical but not arithmetical

verification_methods.well_foundedness: well-foundedness of <_O follows from the inductive definition (each clause produces a successor or limit witness)
verification_methods.Pi_1_1_completeness: encode any Pi_1_1 set into O via tree-of-attempts construction (Kleene 1955)
verification_methods.Church_Kleene_height: every recursive ordinal has an index in O; conversely every index codes a recursive ordinal

Need HyperarithmeticalSet primitive with definability-class slot and well-foundedness verifier. Also need PartialOrdinalNotation primitive (distinct from cap_015's total notation).

entries.object_class: Inner model (K, the Mitchell-Steel core model below a Woodin cardinal)

object_specification.type: K = the core model, an L-like inner model approximating the universe up to the largest large-cardinal it can house

instance.construction_outline: K is built as a union of mice (iterable premice) along the Mitchell-Steel construction; at each stage a new extender E is added if it coheres and a fine-structural soundness condition holds
instance.premouse_definition: a premouse is a structure (J_alpha^{vec E}, in, vec E) where J is the Jensen hierarchy and vec E is a coherent sequence of extenders, with each extender coding a partial elementary embedding
instance.iterability_condition: K is iterable: every countable elementary submodel of K embeds back into K (this is what makes K 'right')
instance.covering_property: if there is no inner model with a Woodin cardinal, then K satisfies weak covering: for every singular cardinal kappa, kappa^+ as computed in K equals (kappa^+)^V
instance.weakening_dependence: the construction of K assumes anti-large-cardinal hypothesis: 'no inner model with a Woodin cardinal'; under stronger LC hypotheses K is replaced by a hod-mouse-like model

structural_metadata.consistency_strength_dependent: the K we get depends on background hypotheses; Steel's K below a Woodin requires anti-Woodin

verification_methods.iterability: Q-structure analysis; Steel's iterability proof for K^c
verification_methods.covering: weak covering via comparison + condensation arguments at singular cardinals
verification_methods.fine_structure: soundness, projecta, standard parameters at every premouse stage

entries.object_class: Recursive ordinal (epsilon_0, the smallest fixed point of alpha |-> omega^alpha)

object_specification.type: epsilon_0 = sup{omega, omega^omega, omega^omega^omega, ...} = least alpha with omega^alpha = alpha

instance.definition_as_supremum: epsilon_0 = sup_n epsilon_0^{(n)} where epsilon_0^{(0)} = omega, epsilon_0^{(n+1)} = omega^{epsilon_0^{(n)}}
instance.cantor_normal_form_below_epsilon_0: every alpha < epsilon_0 has unique CNF: alpha = omega^{beta_1} * c_1 + omega^{beta_2} * c_2 + ... + omega^{beta_k} * c_k with beta_1 > beta_2 > ... > beta_k, c_i in N positive, and each beta_i < alpha (which makes CNF computable recursively)
instance.ordinal_notation: explicit tree-based notation: each ordinal < epsilon_0 is a finite tree with internal nodes labeled by 'omega-power' and leaves by natural numbers; example: omega^{omega+1}*3 + omega*2 + 5
instance.proof_theoretic_role: epsilon_0 is the proof-theoretic ordinal of Peano arithmetic (Gentzen 1936)

structural_metadata.ordinal_notation_index_in_Kleene_O: an explicit recursive notation; well-known computable recursion
structural_metadata.well_foundedness_provable: well-foundedness of the recursive notation system below epsilon_0 is provable in PA + induction up to any alpha < epsilon_0, but not in PA alone (Gentzen)

verification_methods.fixed_point: verify omega^{epsilon_0} = epsilon_0 by passing to the supremum
verification_methods.CNF_uniqueness: induction on ordinal rank below epsilon_0
verification_methods.PA_proof_theoretic: Gentzen's consistency proof of PA uses transfinite induction up to epsilon_0

structural_metadata.gap_with_substrate: Beyond cap_014, we need an ordinal notation system as a first-class object. Need OrdinalNotation primitive with fields tree_representation, recursive_well_order, proof_theoretic_role. Closest existing is generic-recursive-structure which lacks the ordinal-specific axioms.

Canonicalization: epsilon_0 represented as the omega-tower vs as a fixed-point equation should canonicalize to the same notation.

instance.map_equations: p_{n+1} = p_n + K*sin(theta_n) (mod 2*pi); theta_{n+1} = theta_n + p_{n+1} (mod 2*pi)

structural_metadata.K_below_K_critical_implies_invariant_cir: For K=0.5 < 0.9716, KAM theorem guarantees a positive-measure set of invariant circles with Diophantine rotation numbers.

entries.object_class: Geodesic flow on the modular surface SL(2,Z)\H^2

object_specification.type: Continuous-time hyperbolic dynamical system: geodesic flow on the unit tangent bundle of a finite-area hyperbolic orbifold

instance.covering_space: Hyperbolic plane H^2 = {z = x + iy : y > 0} with metric ds^2 = (dx^2 + dy^2)/y^2
instance.deck_group: PSL(2, Z) = <S, T> with S(z) = -1/z, T(z) = z + 1
instance.surface: M = PSL(2, Z) \ H^2 (modular surface; finite-area hyperbolic orbifold with one cusp at infinity and two elliptic points of orders 2 and 3)
instance.unit_tangent_bundle: T^1 M identified with PSL(2, Z) \ PSL(2, R) via Iwasawa decomposition
instance.geodesic_flow_on_PSL_2_R: phi_t(g) = g * diag(e^{t/2}, e^{-t/2}) (right multiplication by the diagonal subgroup A)

structural_metadata.connection_to_continued_fractions: Cross-section to geodesic flow yields the natural extension of the Gauss map; symbolic coding via continued fraction expansion of endpoints.
